name: confirm-dialog
description: An imperative async confirm dialog whose action button owns its own pending and error state: await confirm({ title, action }). The dialog only closes once the action succeeds.

notes: role=alertdialog, aria-modal, labelled by title and described by the message
Focus moves into the dialog on open, is trapped while open, and returns to the trigger on close
Escape and backdrop click cancel (disabled while the action is pending)
On failure the dialog stays open and shows the error as role=alert; the confirm button sets aria-busy while pending

intents: ask the user to confirm a destructive action
async confirm dialog with a pending action
are you sure modal that handles errors
examples: const ok = await confirm({ title: "Delete project?", destructive: true, action: () => api.remove(id) });
